result.stdout:
2.0.0

Usage:  [command] [options] <script>

Arguments:
  script                              Path to CSX script

Options:
  -i|--interactive                    Execute a script and drop into the
                                      interactive mode afterwards.
  -c|--configuration <configuration>  Configuration to use for running the
                                      script [Release/Debug] Default is "Debug"
  -s|--sources <SOURCE>               Specifies a NuGet package source to use
                                      when resolving NuGet packages.
  -p|--cache-path <PATH>              Specify the temporary directory that the
                                      script is built in.
  -d|--debug                          Enables debug output.
  --verbosity                         Set the verbosity level of the command.
                                      Allowed values are t[trace], d[ebug],
                                      i[nfo], w[arning], e[rror], and
                                      c[ritical].
  --no-cache                          Disable caching (Restore and Dll cache)
  --disable-isolated-load-context     Disables isolated assembly load context
  --info                              Displays environmental information
  -?|-h|--help                        Show help information.
  -v|--version                        Show version information.

Commands:
  eval                                Execute CSX code.
  exec                                Run a script from a DLL.
  init                                Creates a sample script along with the
                                      launch.json file needed to launch and
                                      debug the script.
  new                                 Creates a new script file
  publish                             Creates a self contained executable or DLL
                                      from a script
  register                            Register .csx file handler to enable
                                      running scripts directly

Run ' [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.
Starting without a path to a CSX file or a command, starts the REPL (interactive) mode.

commands.payload:
2.0.0

Usage:  [command] [options] <script>

Arguments:
  script                              Path to CSX script

Options:
  -i|--interactive                    Execute a script and drop into the
                                      interactive mode afterwards.
  -c|--configuration <configuration>  Configuration to use for running the
                                      script [Release/Debug] Default is "Debug"
  -s|--sources <SOURCE>               Specifies a NuGet package source to use
                                      when resolving NuGet packages.
  -p|--cache-path <PATH>              Specify the temporary directory that the
                                      script is built in.
  -d|--debug                          Enables debug output.
  --verbosity                         Set the verbosity level of the command.
                                      Allowed values are t[trace], d[ebug],
                                      i[nfo], w[arning], e[rror], and
                                      c[ritical].
  --no-cache                          Disable caching (Restore and Dll cache)
  --disable-isolated-load-context     Disables isolated assembly load context
  --info                              Displays environmental information
  -?|-h|--help                        Show help information.
  -v|--version                        Show version information.

Commands:
  eval                                Execute CSX code.
  exec                                Run a script from a DLL.
  init                                Creates a sample script along with the
                                      launch.json file needed to launch and
                                      debug the script.
  new                                 Creates a new script file
  publish                             Creates a self contained executable or DLL
                                      from a script
  register                            Register .csx file handler to enable
                                      running scripts directly

Run ' [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.
Starting without a path to a CSX file or a command, starts the REPL (interactive) mode.

result.stdout:
Execute CSX code.

Usage:  eval [options] <code>

Arguments:
  code                                               Code to execute.

Options:
  -cwd|--workingdirectory <currentworkingdirectory>  Working directory for the
                                                     code compiler. Defaults to
                                                     current directory.
  -?|-h|--help                                       Show help information.

commands.payload:
Execute CSX code.

Usage:  eval [options] <code>

Arguments:
  code                                               Code to execute.

Options:
  -cwd|--workingdirectory <currentworkingdirectory>  Working directory for the
                                                     code compiler. Defaults to
                                                     current directory.
  -?|-h|--help                                       Show help information.

result.stdout:
Run a script from a DLL.

Usage:  exec [options] <dll>

Arguments:
  dll           Path to DLL based script

Options:
  -d|--debug    Enables debug output.
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

commands.payload:
Run a script from a DLL.

Usage:  exec [options] <dll>

Arguments:
  dll           Path to DLL based script

Options:
  -d|--debug    Enables debug output.
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information.

result.stdout:
Creates a sample script along with the launch.json file needed to launch and debug the script.

Usage:  init [options] <filename>

Arguments:
  filename                                           (Optional) The name of the
                                                     sample script file to be
                                                     created during
                                                     initialization. Defaults to
                                                     'main.csx'

Options:
  -cwd|--workingdirectory <currentworkingdirectory>  Working directory for
                                                     initialization. Defaults to
                                                     current directory.
  -?|-h|--help                                       Show help information.

result.stdout:
Creates a new script file

Usage:  new [options] <filename>

Arguments:
  filename                                           The script file name

Options:
  -cwd|--workingdirectory <currentworkingdirectory>  Working directory the new
                                                     script file to be created.
                                                     Defaults to current
                                                     directory.
  -?|-h|--help                                       Show help information.

commands.payload:
Creates a new script file

Usage:  new [options] <filename>

Arguments:
  filename                                           The script file name

Options:
  -cwd|--workingdirectory <currentworkingdirectory>  Working directory the new
                                                     script file to be created.
                                                     Defaults to current
                                                     directory.
  -?|-h|--help                                       Show help information.

result.stdout:
Creates a self contained executable or DLL from a script

Usage:  publish [options] <filename>

Arguments:
  filename                            The script file name

Options:
  -o|--output                         Directory where the published executable
                                      should be placed. Defaults to a 'publish'
                                      folder in the current directory.
  -n|--name                           The name for the generated DLL (executable
                                      not supported at this time). Defaults to
                                      the name of the script.
  --dll                               Publish to a .dll instead of an
                                      executable.
  -c|--configuration <configuration>  Configuration to use for publishing the
                                      script [Release/Debug]. Default is "Debug"
  -r|--runtime                        The runtime used when publishing the self
                                      contained executable. Defaults to your
                                      current runtime.
  -?|-h|--help                        Show help information.

commands.payload:
Creates a self contained executable or DLL from a script

Usage:  publish [options] <filename>

Arguments:
  filename                            The script file name

Options:
  -o|--output                         Directory where the published executable
                                      should be placed. Defaults to a 'publish'
                                      folder in the current directory.
  -n|--name                           The name for the generated DLL (executable
                                      not supported at this time). Defaults to
                                      the name of the script.
  --dll                               Publish to a .dll instead of an
                                      executable.
  -c|--configuration <configuration>  Configuration to use for publishing the
                                      script [Release/Debug]. Default is "Debug"
  -r|--runtime                        The runtime used when publishing the self
                                      contained executable. Defaults to your
                                      current runtime.
  -?|-h|--help                        Show help information.
